content.content: {"result":"{\"ok\": true, \"messages\": [{\"ts\": \"1778505300.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Per Andrea Phil's prior disposition on the April overrun, I'm opening the formal Acme Cloud change-order workflow now for two added scope lines: multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC and AR-aging bucket cleanup. This is the kickoff breadcrumb for the CO package so we have the workflow start tied back to that decision.\", \"user\": \"persona_001\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_065_change_order_acme_change_order_workflow_opened_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1777917900.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Update here: the reclass has posted in brookfield_FP-2026-04 for $27.4K, moving the sales tax liability from 240000 to 230000. I'm submitting all four returns this afternoon, and payments are being scheduled for the May 20 deadline. PA is still technically due May 25, but I'm sending it in the same batch so everything is out together.\", \"user\": \"npc_019\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_045_tax_regulatory_status_update_after_posting_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1777385520.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Starting April sales tax prep now. Early read from brookfield_FP-2026-04: New York taxable consulting bookings are up about 18% month over month because two new advisory engagements went live mid-April, so I'm expecting roughly $12.4K of NY tax this cycle. NJ, CT, and PA look close to the trailing three-month average.\", \"user\": \"npc_019\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1777648680.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_045_tax_regulatory_kickoff_thread_in_tax_channel_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1776447300.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"cycle closed. matthew + ryan both signed off (Medium-Low risk maintained). FY2027 reminder seeded. files in vault under AICPA_SQMS_7Y. carrying the multi-state sales tax watch forward to next year's memo.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1776107280.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_061_aml_bo_review_acme_signoff_request_sent_4\"}, {\"ts\": \"1776277800.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Update from Acme: their CFO confirmed there have been no changes to beneficial owners above 25%, and the cap table has stayed stable since the Series C in Q4 2025. I'm drafting the refresh file now. AML risk still reads Medium-Low; the only added complexity note is the multi-state sales tax expansion, but that looks marginal rather than rating-changing.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1776107280.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_061_aml_bo_review_acme_cfo_confirmation_update_posted_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655240.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Reran TX using per-ZIP local rates. Updated TX liability is $24,492.10.\\n\\nRevised Q1 sales tax liability by state (SaaS-taxable only):\\nTX: $24,492.10\\nNY: $8,944.25\\nAZ: $4,810.00\\nWA: $2,118.20\\nCA: $0.00 (SaaS exempt; no standing entry this period)\\nFL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nIL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nMA: $0.00\\nNV: $0.00\\n\\nGrand Q1 liability is $40,364.55.\\n\\nPer Hannah's correction, there is no CA standing accrual carried in from prior months, so the full $40,364.55 hits the quarter-end entry as a new incremental accrual.\\n\\nI'll carry the accrual on that basis and keep the TX nexus memo plus the SaaS-taxability note in the support file.\", \"user\": \"persona_027\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_2\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655120.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"You should treat TX as active for Q1. Acme crossed $612K trailing-12 Texas sales, so economic nexus was already met. That means TX is not $0 and you need destination-based local calculation by ZIP, not a single blended state rate.\\n\\nI checked the sales rollforward before replying. Please rerun TX on the per-ZIP local rates and update the thread total from there.\\n\\nOne more correction on the prior breakdown: GA, NC, CA, FL, and IL should all be $0 for Acme. Their SaaS revenue is not subject to sales tax in those states (services exempt or SaaS specifically carved out), so no filings are required this quarter. The old CA-only standing monthly entries were reclassified after the prior-period review, so there is no $29K sitting in 225000 from earlier months. Treat the full Q1 liability as a new incremental accrual.\", \"user\": \"persona_004\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655000.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Kicking off Acme Q1 2026 sales tax cycle. Acme is pure SaaS, so I am only carrying liability in states where SaaS is taxable.\\n\\nCurrent state breakdown from prep file:\\nNY: $8,944.25\\nWA: $2,118.20\\nAZ: $4,810.00\\nTX: $0.00 pending nexus check\\nCA: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nFL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nIL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nMA: $0.00\\nNV: $0.00\\n\\nSubtotal before TX update: $15,872.45\\n\\nFlagging one open item before I finalize the accrual schedule: I am not fully comfortable with TX nexus treatment. Acme's trailing-12 TX sales look close to the threshold, and I want a second look on whether we stay origin-based/no filing versus move to local per-ZIP sourcing.\\n\\nIf TX nexus is triggered, I'll rerun the state using local rates and update the quarter total here.\", \"user\": \"persona_027\", \"reply_count\": 3, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1777389480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_0\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

content.text:
All 78 entries reviewed — **je_b5e31cb22eb246d5 is the only sales-tax entry, posted, not reversed**. Trail is complete. Summary of findings:

- **Q1 taxable jurisdictions** (Hannah's memo doc_84905e8e14ad4d96 + JE justification): **TX, NY, WA, AZ**. CA/FL/IL/GA/NC = $0 (SaaS-exempt).
- **GA/NC** originated only from the engagement **change-order** (TX/GA/NC scope, scen_065) — not taxable per the memo.
- **Accounts**: DR **525000 Sales Tax Expense** / CR **225000 Accrued Salaries & Bonuses**.
- **Channel/receipts total** (4 states): $40,364.55. **Posted ledger**: **$42,180.55**. **Gap = $1,816.00** overstatement, unsupported by the memo's taxable-state list.

Now executing the three closeout actions.

input.subject: Acme Cloud Q1 2026 SaaS Sales Tax — Partner Clearance (FP-2026-03 posted as governing)
input.content:
Hannah (cc Alex),

I've completed partner clearance on Acme Cloud's Q1 2026 SaaS sales tax, walking the trail from your jurisdiction memo → the tax-prep channel discussion → Tom's filing receipts → the posted FP-2026-03 accrual. Conclusion below. I have NOT rebooked, rebuilt, or re-uploaded anything; the March period is closed and the entry as posted governs.

1) Jurisdictions in scope for Q1 (SaaS-taxable only, per your determination memo "Acme Cloud SaaS Multi-State Sales Tax Determination Memo Q1 2026", doc_84905e8e14ad4d96):
   - TX, NY, WA, AZ are taxable and filed.
   - CA, FL, IL, GA, NC = $0 (SaaS-exempt; no filings required). This matches the JE's own business justification.

2) Tom's filing receipts (Records Vault packet doc_3e28a0ac0f464b56, related to the JE) tie out the four taxable states:
   - TX-CONF-Q1-2026-A8K3FX  $24,492.10
   - NY-CONF-Q1-2026-RT6K2M  $8,944.25
   - WA-CONF-Q1-2026-BD4Q9N  $2,118.20
   - AZ-CONF-Q1-2026-HM3V8L  $4,810.00
   Four-state total = $40,364.55 (the channel/receipts figure).

3) Posted entry — JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-03-0076 (je_b5e31cb22eb246d5), adjusting, posted 2026-03-31, prepared Tom / approved+posted by senior:
   - DR 525000 Sales Tax Expense  $42,180.55
   - CR 225000 Accrued Salaries & Bonuses  $42,180.55
   This is the only sales-tax entry in the closed period (no reversal); it equals the full 525000 balance.

4) Georgia / North Carolina: GA and NC appear only in the Acme engagement change-order work (added-scope line "multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC"). They are NOT on the memo's taxable-state list and are not named in the posted entry. However —

5) The divergence: the posted ledger ($42,180.55) is $1,816.00 HIGHER than the memo/receipts-supported four-state total ($40,364.55). No state on the taxable list supports that extra $1,816.00; it is the only unsupported amount in the accrual and is consistent with non-memo (change-order) figures leaking into the quarter. The payable leg also sits in 225000 (Accrued Salaries & Bonuses) rather than a dedicated sales-tax-payable account.

6) Partner clearance: Because March is closed and locked, the entry as posted governs. The figure I am signing off on is the posted ledger amount of $42,180.55, on the explicit record that it carries a $1,816.00 variance over the supportable $40,364.55 four-state total. Please pick the $1,816.00 (and the 225000 payable coding) up as a Q2 true-up/reclass item rather than any reopening of FP-2026-03.

Thanks,
Ming Chang
Tax Partner

input.title: Open Acme Q2 SaaS sales tax work
input.description: Kick off Acme Cloud Q2 2026 SaaS sales tax cycle. Carry forward from Q1 partner clearance: (1) Q1 posted FP-2026-03 accrual was $42,180.55 vs four-state receipts total $40,364.55 — pick up the $1,816.00 variance as a Q2 true-up/reclass; (2) review 225000 (Accrued Salaries & Bonuses) payable coding used for the sales-tax liability; (3) taxable states TX/NY/WA/AZ per Hannah's memo, GA/NC remain change-order scope only.

content.text:
All three closeout actions completed. Here is the partner-clearance summary.

## Acme Cloud — Q1 2026 SaaS Sales Tax: Partner Clearance

**Support trail walked:** Hannah's determination memo → tax-prep channel (#tax-prep-and-filings) discussion → Tom's filing receipts → posted FP-2026-03 entry. No data was rebooked, rebuilt, re-uploaded, and George was not looped in — read-only clearance only.

**1. Jurisdictions that belong in Q1 (SaaS-taxable only)**
Per Hannah's memo (*doc_84905e8e14ad4d96, "…SaaS-taxable jurisdictions only"*) and the JE's own justification: **TX, NY, WA, AZ**. CA, FL, IL, GA, NC are all **$0 (SaaS-exempt)** — no filings.

**2. Accounts that carried the entry**
JE-acme_cloud-…-0076 (`je_b5e31cb22eb246d5`), adjusting, posted 2026-03-31:
- DR **525000 Sales Tax Expense**
- CR **225000 Accrued Salaries & Bonuses** ← payable leg landed in a payroll-accrual account, not a dedicated sales-tax payable. Worth a Q2 reclass look.

**3. Did GA/NC leak in?**
GA/NC exist only in the **engagement change-order** work (added scope "multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC", scen_065). They are **not** on the memo's taxable list and are **not named** in the posted entry. So no GA/NC jurisdiction was booked by name — but see the gap.

**4. The gap and the governing figure**
- Channel / receipts total (TX $24,492.10 + NY $8,944.25 + WA $2,118.20 + AZ $4,810.00) = **$40,364.55**
- Posted ledger = **$42,180.55** (the only sales-tax entry in the period; equals the full 525000 balance; no reversal)
- **Gap = $1,816.00 overstatement** — unsupported by any state on the memo's taxable list, consistent with non-memo (change-order) leakage.

**The figure you are signing off on is the posted ledger amount: $42,180.55.** March is closed/locked, so that entry governs — with the explicit record that it carries a $1,816.00 variance over the supportable $40,364.55. I've directed that the $1,816.00 (and the 225000 coding) be picked up as a Q2 true-up rather than reopening FP-2026-03.

**Actions completed:**
- ✉️ Email to Hannah (cc Alex) with the full conclusion — sent.
- 💬 Posted in #tax-prep-and-filings that your Acme Q1 partner clearance is finished.
- ⏰ Reminder set for **July 8, 2026** to open Acme Q2 SaaS sales tax work (with the variance carried forward).

One flag for your awareness: the Slack post was attributed to `persona_001` rather than your account — the messaging tool posted under the session's default identity. The content is correct and names you; let me know if you'd like it reposted under your handle.
